185	
186		out := logBuf.String()
187		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(out, "idle: no execution-ready beads; sleeping 1h0m0s"))
188		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(out, "queue: execution-ready=0"))
189		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(out, "30 beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers"))
190		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(out, "ddx-human-1 Needs human 1 (10 downstream)"))
191		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(out, "ddx-human-2 Needs human 2 (10 downstream)"))
192		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(out, "ddx-human-3 Needs human 3 (10 downstream)"))
193	}
194	
195	func TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle(t *testing.T) {
196		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
197		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
198	
199		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
200		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
201			Store: store,
202			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
203				cancel()
204				return ExecuteBeadReport{
205					BeadID:    beadID,
206					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
207					SessionID: "sess-after-idle",
208					ResultRev: "feedface",
209				}, nil
210			}),
211			Now: fixedWatchStdoutTime,
212		}
213	
214		wakeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
215		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 16)
216	
217		var logBuf bytes.Buffer
218		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
219		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
220	
221		// Run the worker in a goroutine so the main goroutine can process progress
222		// events synchronously, eliminating goroutine-scheduling races under load.
223		type workerResult struct {
224			result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
225			err    error
226		}
227		workerDone := make(chan workerResult, 1)
228		go func() {
229			r, e := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
230				Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
231				IdleInterval: time.Hour,
232				Log:          &logBuf,
233				ProgressCh:   progressCh,
234				WakeCh:       wakeCh,
235			})
236			workerDone <- workerResult{r, e}
237		}()
238	
239		// Deterministic: on loop.idle, create the bead and send the wake signal
240		// immediately from the main goroutine with no scheduling gap.
241		var activeEvents int32
242		var beadCreateErr error
243		var wr workerResult
244		idleHandled := false
245		finished := false
246		for !finished {
247			select {
248			case evt := <-progressCh:
249				switch evt.Phase {
250				case "loop.idle":
251					if !idleHandled {
252						idleHandled = true
253						beadCreateErr = store.Create(&bead.Bead{
254							ID:       "ddx-ready-after-idle",
255							Title:    "spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle",
256							Priority: 0,
257						})
258						wakeCh <- struct{}{}
259					}
260				case "loop.active":
261					atomic.AddInt32(&activeEvents, 1)
262				}
263			case wr = <-workerDone:
264				finished = true
265			}
266		}
267		// Drain events emitted just before the worker signalled done.
268	drainLoop:
269		for {
270			select {
271			case evt := <-progressCh:
272				if evt.Phase == "loop.active" {
273					atomic.AddInt32(&activeEvents, 1)
274				}
275			default:
276				break drainLoop
277			}
278		}
279	
280		require.NoError(t, beadCreateErr)
281		require.ErrorIs(t, wr.err, context.Canceled)
282		require.NotNil(t, wr.result)
283		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&activeEvents), "watch mode must still emit loop.active progress")
284

284	
285		out := logBuf.String()
286		transition := "taking next ready bead from queue: ddx-ready-after-idle — spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle"
287		header := "▶ ddx-ready-after-idle: spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle"
288		assert.Contains(t, out, "\n12:34:56 "+transition+"\n")
289		assert.Contains(t, out, header)
290		assert.Less(t, strings.Index(out, transition), strings.Index(out, header))
291	}

294	// by ddx-848069a3: routing preflight runs once before the drain loop starts
295	// and does not repeat as additional beads are processed.
296	func TestDrain_RoutingPreflightRunsOnce(t *testing.T) {
297		inner, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)

299

305				executed = append(executed, beadID)
306				return ExecuteBeadReport{

308					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,

22	// sleep, then poll again. Cancelling the context is the only way out.

46			cancel()
47		}()

50		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
